title: ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. alismoides
intro: Ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. alismoides, also known as Alisma-leaved Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and fight infections. The health benefits of Ranunculus pyrenaeus subsp. alismoides include its potential to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as eczema, acne, and other skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to create infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
